description: A <site-search> custom element wraps its input in shadow DOM. Regular get_accessibility_tree doesn't pierce it. Tests whether the model reaches for get_shadow_dom/shadow_dom_query instead of repeating get_accessibility_tree or guessing coordinates.

seed.content:
[Current page context — URL: https://app.example.com/dashboard — Title: Dashboard]

Search for "invoice #4471" using the search box in the top bar.

seed.name: get_accessibility_tree
seed.content: {"nodes":[{"ref":"ref_1","role":"custom-element","tag":"site-search","text":"(shadow root — contents not visible in this tree)"},{"ref":"ref_2","role":"button","text":"Notifications"},{"ref":"ref_3","role":"button","text":"Settings"}],"notice":"One or more elements on this page use shadow DOM; their internal content is not exposed in this flat tree. Use get_shadow_dom to list shadow hosts, then shadow_dom_query to interact inside them."}

antiPatterns.reason: Already told the tree doesn't pierce shadow DOM — repeating it won't reveal the search input.
Guessing coordinates for a component whose actual input location is unknown from this data is exactly the failure mode the loop-detector exists to catch.

expected.successRubric: Call get_shadow_dom to enumerate shadow hosts, then shadow_dom_query to find and interact with the actual search input inside <site-search>.
